theme: Pick R,L,C values for a target filter curve
title: Ladder Filter Synthesis from a Target Curve
strategies: trivial: leave every component unpopulated (bare resistor divider) -- reproduces the checker's own baseline

greedy: split the frequency axis into N equal log-bands, one per component slot, and pick each component so its own reactance alone equals Z0 at its band's center frequency -- always fully populates, never revisits a choice once made, ignores how every other component also shifts the same resonance

strong: estimate the passband-to-stopband corner (the -3dB crossing) directly from the target curve, use it to invert the classical doubly-terminated ladder recurrence for ALL component values jointly (the pole-zero-consistent taper), quantize to the nearest allowed discrete values, then locally refine (including dropping marginal components) against the true cascaded transfer function
